account.name: Will Brown
account.notes: RLHF/alignment researcher, active on ML Twitter

account.name: Nathan Lambert
account.notes: Interconnects newsletter, ex-HuggingFace, AI2 RLHF lead

about.bio:
Interconnects is a newsletter covering the latest AI models, the methods used to train them, and the trajectory of the technology. 1 to 3 times a week you’ll receive a mix of essays, model reviews, interviews with researchers, and surveys of the open model ecosystem. Interconnects is founded and operated by
Nathan Lambert
— a senior research scientist and post-training lead at the Allen Institute for AI (Ai2). At the non-profit Ai2, Nathan trains open language models (
Olmos
) and shares cutting edge research. Interconnects serves his broader goals of creating a more informed AI ecosystem built on openness and transparency.
Interconnects grew out of a longer history of Nathan trying to share educational resources in the AI space. Soon after the release of ChatGPT, Nathan transitioned a hobbyist approach to writing into a weekly commitment with over 300 posts to date. This is now deeply intertwined with his research practice and taste — trying to transfer the feeling of understanding a cutting edge research topic in AI to his readers.
Nathan is also known for a series of prominent research works in post-training language models, such as
Olmo
,
Tülu 2 or 3
,
RewardBench
,
Zephyr-Beta
, the
Open LLM Leaderboard
,
Molmo
, or the first textbook on
RLHF
. Core topics of Interconnects follow Nathan’s work closely, from early days on
reinforcement learning from human feedback
(RLHF) to the
reasoning models
of today, with a particular focus on
open-source AI
and
open models
. To understand Nathan’s approach to research, you can read more on his
path into AI
.
Many of the most popular pieces on Interconnects are frontier model reviews, such as recapping the
DeepSeek R1 recipe
or explaining
o3’s odd over-optimization
. For some more timeless pieces, consider exploring:
All essays and model reviews are voice-overed by Nathan and available in
podcast
feeds along with the interviews.
The most valuable thing is to
subscribe
and spread the word with friends. On Substack, likes, com

account.name: Ryan Lopopolo
account.notes: OpenAI Frontier. Creator of 'Harness Engineering' concept. Built Symphony (Elixir-based multi-agent orchestration). Previously at Snowflake, Brick, Stripe, Citadel.

account.name: Boris Cherny
account.notes: Creator and head of Claude Code at Anthropic. Author of 'Programming TypeScript' (O'Reilly). Believes 'coding is solved'. Previously at Instagram.

about.bio:
About
I’m Boris and this is my blog.
I am a Software Engineer at Anthropic, where I created
Claude Code
. Before that I was at Instagram, where I prototyped and scaled the next big ideas for product & infra. A few years ago I wrote a book:
O’Reilly’s Programming TypeScript
. I live in San Francisco. In my free time you can find me reading, running, and salting things.
Find me on
Threads
,
X
,
Github
, and
LinkedIn
, or get in touch at boris at this domain dot com.

topics: NPM and NodeJS should do more to make ES Modules easy to use
Dinosaur Food: 100 million year old foods we still eat today
Data Management on the Web Still Sucks
React+TypeScript: Use unions of objects for props
13 Tips for Writing a Technical Book

account.name: Mike Krieger
account.notes: CPO at Anthropic. Co-founder of Instagram and Artifact. Discussed how Anthropic uses AI to write 90-95% of code. Focus on product differentiation over raw coding speed.

account.name: Addy Osmani
account.notes: Engineering Lead at Google (Chrome & Web Platform). Wrote extensively on agentic coding, conductors vs orchestrators, self-improving coding agents, Claude Code swarms.

account.name: Dax Raad
account.notes: Creator of OpenCode (open source AI coding agent for terminal), SST founder. Focus on TUI-first developer experience, multi-agent parallel sessions.

account.name: Akira (Random Labs)
account.notes: Creator of Slate — first swarm-native coding agent using Thread Weaving architecture for persistent context in long-horizon engineering. Random Labs co-founder, YC S24. Advocate of RLM for coding.

account.name: Alex Zhang
account.notes: MIT CSAIL PhD student (advised by Omar Khattab & Tim Kraska). Proposed Recursive Language Models (RLMs). GPU MODE community core. Runs GPU MODE leaderboard with NVIDIA. Works on KernelBench, VideoGameBench, SWE-bench Multimodal.

account.name: Daniel Han
account.notes: Creator of Unsloth — makes fine-tuning & RL (GRPO, LoRA, QLoRA) accessible with 2x speed and 70% less VRAM. Collaborates with HuggingFace & NVIDIA on RL courses. 100K+ models trained with Unsloth on HF.

about.bio:
About
Hey there! We started as a team of two brothers!

account.name: Florian Brand
account.notes: Prime Intellect engineer. Contributor to PRIME-RL, Verifiers, Environments Hub. Works on distributed RL training infrastructure and open superintelligence stack.
